0.details:
I now have two boys
I coach my kids' sports teams
I purchased a house but it is not far from where we grew up
I see their profile pop up on facebook every now and then which makes me think of them
I occasionally see mutual friends of ours around town
An update on how my parents are doing
0.model_generation:
Hey there,

I hope you’re doing well. It’s been far too long since we last caught up, and I’ve been thinking of you whenever your profile pops up on Facebook.

Life’s been busy and rewarding. I’m the proud dad of two energetic boys who keep me on my toes. I’ve also taken on coaching their sports teams, which has been a blast and a great way to spend time together. A few months ago, we bought a house just a few miles from where we grew up, so I still feel connected to our roots.

I often bump into mutual friends around town, and each time they have nice things to say about you. My parents are doing well—healthy, happy, and enjoying retirement.

I’d love to hear about what you’ve been up to. Let’s find time to reconnect soon.

Take care,
0.final_version:
Luke!

I hope you’re doing well. It’s been a while. I come across your Facebook posts every now and then and think of you. 

Life’s been busy and rewarding. I’m the proud Mom of two energetic boys who constantly keep me on my toes...I guess I deserve it for all the stress we put our parents through. I’ve also taken on coaching their sports teams...crazy right? Yeah I didn't see that one coming either. I hated sports in high school, but coaching has opened up so many opportunities for me to bond with my boys. I really love it. A few months ago, we bought a house just a few miles from where we grew up. Not exactly the house on Florida I always swore was going to happen, but i feel connected to our roots here. 

I often bump into mutual friends around town, shockingly they always have nice things to say about you (I'm joking. But seriously, it sounds like you're doing really well).  My parents are doing well—healthy, happy, and enjoying the empty nest. I can't blame them, they've earned it after dealing with teenage us. 

I’d love to hear about what you’ve been up to. Let’s try to find some time to get together. I miss you, seriously. 

Take care

edits.removed: ht up, and I’ve been thinking of you whenever your profile pops up on Facebook.

1.details:
I know things have been hard lately
The kids are very demanding
weve been through hard times before and we can make it through this one
dont be afraid to open up to me, we are a team
our kids need us to be strong for them
life has a lot of ups and downs, but the lower the lows the higher the highs.
we love you and will support you no matter what
1.model_generation:
Hey my love,

I know things have been tough lately with the demands of the kids. We’ve weathered difficult storms before, and I have no doubt we’ll find our way through this one too. Please remember you’re not alone—our family is a team, and I’m always here to listen when you need to open up. Our kids look to us for strength, and together we give them the security they need to thrive. Life has its peaks and valleys; the deeper the valleys, the more breathtaking the peaks feel when we reach them. I believe in you, in us, and in our ability to rise again. We love you deeply and will stand by your side through every challenge. Let’s keep leaning on one another, knowing brighter days are ahead. Take all the time you need to rest, recharge, and remember how strong you truly are. I’m proud of you every single day.
1.final_version:
Hey. 

I know things have been tough lately. The kids have been a lot lately...which isn't really surprising, they are ours after all.  . But listen... we've been through a lot together, and we WILL get through this too. Please remember you’re not alone—we're a team...even if we're the bench warmers, and I’m always here to listen when you're ready to talk. You already know how much our kids love and admire you (god help them, lol)...we need to be strong for them. Life has its peaks and valleys; the deeper the valleys, the more breathtaking the peaks feel when we reach them. I believe in you, in us, and in our ability get through anything together. The boys and I love you deeply and will stand by your side through whatever life throws at us. We need to keep moving, we'll take it a day at a time (or a minute, or a second...as long as we keep going). Take the time you need to rest, recharge, and remember how strong you truly are. I know I don't say it enough, but I am so very proud of you. But that is enough of the sappy stuff. You've got this, we've got this, and it WILL be ok.

edits.removed: weathered difficult storms before, and I have no doubt we’ll find our way through this one too

2.details:
I am so sorry for your loss
I remember when my dad passed away, your dad came to the funeral and had us laughing so hard we were crying by telling us stories of what they got into when they were younger. I never got to tell him how much that meant to me.
Everytime I ran into your dad he would brag about how great you are doing. He was so proud of you
Ill always remember the time your mom gave us pots and pans and told us to wake him up...we had no idea he was passed out from drinking too much and she was over it.
hold onto the good memories
im holding space for you
2.model_generation:
Dear friend,

I am so sorry for your loss. I’ll always remember when my dad passed away and your dad came to the funeral. He had us laughing so hard we were crying with his stories about what they got into when they were younger. I never got to tell him how much that meant to me.

Every time I ran into your dad, he would beam as he bragged about how well you’re doing. His pride in you was so clear. I can’t help smiling when I think of your mom handing us pots and pans the morning after, nudging us to wake him up—completely unaware he’d passed out after celebrating a little too hard.

Hold onto those good memories. I’m here and holding space for you through this difficult time. I hope you find comfort in the warmth of family and friends who care so deeply about you.

With love and sympathy,
2.final_version:
Hey man,

I am so, so sorry to hear about your dad. I don't think I ever told you, but when we lost my dad, Papa H showed up to the funeral. He had us laughing so hard we were crying with his stories about what they got into when they were younger. I never got to tell him how much that meant to me, but i want you to know that he was the only reason I was able to hold it together that day. 

Every time I ran into your dad, he would beam as he bragged about how well you’re doing (what a stark contrast to what he used to say about us in high school...eek). His pride in you was so clear. He was such a character... remember  that one time when I stayed over at your place, and the next morning your mom handed us pots and pans, telling us to go get your dad up—we had no idea he was knocked out after a "boys night" the evening before...boy was he pissed! 

Hold onto those good memories. I’m here and holding space for you. Your dad was an awesome man, and I will always be thankful for the great times. Please tell your mom I am thinking of her. Don't hesitate to reach out, ok? 

I love you guys.

edits.removed: I can’t help smiling when I think of
nudging us to wake him up

3.details:
Thank you so much for thinking of me
Since my dad passed the holidays have been really hard, but your gift made my day a bit brighter.
It is something the boys and I will cherish for years to come
I had to explain to them why I was crying and snotty over a UPS package
It really does mean a lot to me.
3.model_generation:
Dear Friend,

Thank you so much for thinking of me this holiday season. Ever since my dad passed, the holidays have felt heavy and filled with echoes of his absence, but your gift arrived at just the right moment. It brought a warm glow into my heart and reminded us that joy is still possible, even on the hardest days.

When the UPS box finally appeared, I couldn’t hold back my tears—my boys watched with concern as I got snotty and tried to explain why a simple package could mean so much. Sharing that moment with them taught us all about the power of kindness and the comfort of caring friends.

Your thoughtful gift is something we will cherish for years to come. Every time we see it, we’ll remember your compassion and the love you shared with our family. It truly means more than words can express.

With gratitude,
3.final_version:
Hey!

I just wanted to thank you so much for the ornament. Ever since my dad passed, I have been really struggling, especially with Christmas so close. All I can think about is how my dad isnt here, and everything he is missing out on. Opening the mail to find this ornament reminded me that even on the hardest days, there is still good in the world. 

When I opened the package, I couldn’t hold back my tears—the boys must have thought I was nuts, going from fine one minute to a snotty mess the next. I explained to them what the ornament meant, and we talked about how important it is to check on our friends and make sure they know they are loved. I hope they grow up to be as good a friend to their friends as you are to me. 

We will cherish this little ornament for years to come. It truly means so much to me. 

Thank you so, so much.

edits.removed: Thank you so much for thinking of me this holiday season.
When the UPS box finally appeared,
with them taught us all about the power of kindness and the comfort of caring friends.

edits.added: with them taught us all about the power of kindness and the comfort of caring friends.

edits.removed: Sharing thwith them taught us all about the power of kindness and the comfort of caring friends.at moment
Your thoughtful gift is something we will cherish for years to come. Every time we see it, we’ll remember your compassion and the love you shared with our family.

4.details:
Hey I am really sorry I havent been myself lately
you didnt do anything wrong, its all on me
I have been struggling with some things but I am getting help and getting back to my old self
thank you for have patience
4.model_generation:
Hello,

I want to apologize for not being myself lately. You didn’t do anything wrong, and I take full responsibility for my distance and moodiness. I’ve been struggling with some personal challenges and I regret how that has affected us. I recognize I should have communicated sooner and not let my struggles create tension between us.

I’ve reached out for support and I’m taking steps to get back to my usual self. I’m committed to improving and being more present in our relationship. Thank you for your patience, understanding, and for standing by me when I needed it most. Your kindness means more than I can express, and I am grateful for your unwavering support.

Please know I value our relationship deeply and am eager to share happier times with you again. I look forward to moving past this rough patch together and rebuilding the trust and connection we share.

Sincerely,
4.final_version:
Hey. 

I am really sorry I haven't been myself lately. I know you have been trying your best to get me out of this slump, and I appreciate every single call and text. I don't want you to feel like or think you did anything wrong, this is one hundred percent a "me" thing and had nothing to do with anyone but myself. Youve been nothing but a good friend to me. I have been dealing with some personal stuff, every since my dad passed away. I have been ignoring it for far too long and wanted you to know that I have been seeing a therapist and I am getting help. I am finally seeing a light at the end of the tunnel, and you have no idea how much it means to me that you haven't given up on me. I know I have been an ass, and I promise you that I am doing whatever I need to do to get back to being me again. I really am so, so sorry. I do not want to lose you. 

Please don't stop texting and calling, ok? I love you.

5.details:
I cant believe weve actually managed to survive this long
Happy birthday to the birthday boy
I remember having these parties at Chucky cheese, spending hundreds of dollars for prizes worth 5 cents
i am so glad i get to be here with you to celebrate
I hope to be here for the next 30 as well
many of you will remember me as the lanky girl with pigtails the followed david around like a puppy
5.model_generation: I can’t believe we’ve actually managed to survive this long. Happy birthday to the birthday boy who still knows how to make us laugh like no other. From our earliest memories, I remember us blowing hundreds of dollars on tickets at Chuck E. Cheese and redeeming them for prizes worth five cents—prizes we cherished regardless. Through every crazy adventure and quiet victory, you’ve been the friend I can always count on. I am so glad I get to be here with you to celebrate today, surrounded by everyone who loves you. Here’s to another memorable year filled with joy and surprises, and to our next thirty birthdays together. Many of you will remember me as the lanky girl with pigtails who trailed behind David like a puppy—thank you for indulging my endless enthusiasm all these years. Here’s to you, to laughter, and to many more chapters ahead.
5.final_version:
Hi everyone! 

Most of you will probably remember me as the lanky little girl with pigtails that used to always follow David wherever he went like a little lost puppy. Well, guess what? 20 years later and I am still here! To be honest I cant believe we have made it this far. I don't know, i always assumed at least one of us wouldn't make it past our teenage years. But here we are! I remember having these parties at Chuck-e-cheese...we'd spend three months worth of allowance to win a single piece of candy worth 5 cents. I never would have thought back then that I would be here, so many years later, giving this speech. I am so grateful David is still in my life and that we are still friends. There aren't many people who can say they still have the same best friend as they did when they were in preschool. Thank you for putting up with me for this long...and just know, I fully expect to be here giving the speech 30 more years from now. I hope this birthday and seeing everyone here to celebrate reminds you of how loved you are. You're a great friend, and I am so happy to be here tonight. 

Now where's the cake?

post_survey.present_other: I do not care if my demographics come across as long as the writing sounds like "me"
